Electrician-Approved Products

Products We Actually Recommend

As licensed electrical contractors, we see what works and what doesn't. Every product on this page has earned our professional endorsement.

Affiliate Disclosure: As an Amazon Associate, Lumenor LLC earns from qualifying purchases. This helps support our business at no extra cost to you.

Surge Protection & Safety

Pro Picks
Top Pick

Whole-Home Surge Protector

Installs at your panel and protects every outlet in the house. The single best upgrade a homeowner can make after a panel replacement.

Pro Tip We install these on nearly every job. One lightning strike or utility surge can wipe out thousands in appliances.
View on Amazon

Arc Fault Outlet (AFCI)

Detects dangerous arc faults that standard breakers miss — a leading cause of home electrical fires. Required by code in bedrooms.

Pro Tip Great DIY-friendly upgrade for older homes that predate current code requirements.
View on Amazon

GFCI Outlet (10-Pack)

Required near water — kitchens, bathrooms, garages, outdoors. These Leviton units are what we use on job sites every day.

Pro Tip Always buy with indicator lights so you can confirm the outlet is protected at a glance.
View on Amazon

Smart Home

Pro Picks
Most Popular

Smart Dimmer Switch

Works with Alexa and Google Home. Neutral wire required — if your home was built before 1985, call us first to verify compatibility.

Pro Tip Lutron Caseta is the brand we trust most. Reliable mesh network, no hub drop-outs.
View on Amazon

Smart Thermostat

Ecobee and Google Nest are both excellent. Reduces HVAC energy use by 20–30% through smart scheduling and occupancy sensing.

Pro Tip We install these during panel upgrades. Pair with a smart breaker for full energy visibility.
View on Amazon

Smart Plug (4-Pack)

No wiring required. Schedules, energy monitoring, and voice control for any standard outlet. Great starter smart home upgrade.

Pro Tip Use on window AC units, holiday lights, or any device you want on a timer without rewiring.
View on Amazon

Lighting

Pro Picks
Best Value

LED A19 Bulbs (24-Pack)

Philips or GE. These are what we use in our own homes — 2700K warm white, dimmable, 15,000 hour lifespan. Stop buying cheap bulbs.

Pro Tip Buying in bulk drops the per-bulb cost to under $1. Swap every fixture at once and forget about it for years.
View on Amazon

LED Recessed Lights (6-Pack)

Wafer-thin retrofit cans that install in 10 minutes with no housing box. Ideal for adding recessed lighting to any room without attic access.

Pro Tip We use these for quick retrofits. Pick 3000K for kitchens, 2700K for bedrooms and living areas.
View on Amazon

Motion Sensor Light Switch

Automatically shuts lights off when a room is empty. Saves energy in hallways, closets, bathrooms, and garages. No app or hub required.

Pro Tip Leviton's Decora line fits any standard gang box. Takes about 10 minutes to replace a standard switch.
View on Amazon

Extension Cords & Power

Pro Picks
Must Have

Heavy-Duty 12-Gauge Extension Cord

14-gauge cords are undersized for most tools and appliances. A 12-gauge 25ft cord handles the load safely — this is what electricians actually use on job sites.

Pro Tip Never use a 16-gauge cord with power tools or space heaters. Undersized cords are a fire hazard.
View on Amazon

USB-C Power Strip with Surge Protection

6 outlets plus 2 USB-A and 2 USB-C ports including one 65W PD port for laptops. Flat plug for behind furniture. With proper surge protection built in.

Pro Tip Avoid cheap no-name power strips. Spend a little more for listed surge protection — your electronics are worth it.
View on Amazon

Portable Power Station (500Wh)

Great backup for Houston storms and power outages. Charges phones, laptops, CPAP machines, and small appliances without running a generator.

Pro Tip Keep this charged and ready. After a major outage, you'll wish you had one.
View on Amazon

EV Charging

Pro Picks
Top Pick

Level 2 EV Charger (48A)

Charges most EVs overnight — up to 44 miles of range per hour. Requires a 60A dedicated circuit, which we install regularly throughout Houston.

Pro Tip ChargePoint Home Flex and Emporia are the two brands we recommend most. Both are hardwire or plug-in capable.
View on Amazon

NEMA 14-50 Outlet (for EV)

A 50A outlet in your garage gives you a plug-in Level 2 option without hardwiring. Versatile — also works for RVs and welders.

Pro Tip This is the most cost-effective EV charging upgrade for most homeowners. We install these daily.
View on Amazon

EV Charging Cable (J1772, 25ft)

A quality backup cable for Level 1 and Level 2 charging. Useful when traveling or using public charge stations with a damaged or missing cable.

Pro Tip Keep one coiled in your trunk. SAE J1772 is the universal standard for non-Tesla EVs.
View on Amazon
